channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I think a good Product Manager isn't someone who knows everything, but someone who connects everything
item.description: I think a good Product Manager isn't someone who knows everything, but someone who connects everything There was a detail that made me pause when learning about Product Manager skills: this role...

item.title: The Most Common QA Engineer Interview Question Also Has the Lowest Score

item.title: On Flock License Plate Tracking Cameras
item.description: A recent story of a writer who was mistakenly identified, tracked, and arrested using data from Flock cameras has gone viral. The New Jersey plates that were allegedly stolen from the LA dealer...

item.title: VIDRAFT Releases Fully Open Foundation Model Aether-7B-5Attn: Weights, Code, Data, and Training Logs — All Public

item.title: PDF Tamper Detection API for Java: Spring Boot Integration Guide
item.description: Originally published at htpbe.tech. The version on htpbe.tech stays in sync with the latest detection algorithm — refer to it for the canonical text. PDF fraud is a backend problem, and in...

item.title: Cloud Migration Strategies: The 6 Rs
item.description: Migration isn't one decision, it's hundreds "We're moving to the cloud" is the start of a conversation, not a plan. A real estate of 200 applications doesn't have one migration strategy, it has up...

item.title: How proprietary formats have become Microsoft’s main tool for lock-in!
item.description: The Architecture of Lock-in: Evaluating Proprietary Formats as Strategic Barriers The evolution of enterprise software ecosystems has been defined by a fundamental tension between interoperability...

item.title: Why I Designed an Invite-Code Board With No Cash Rewards
item.description: The first real design decision I made for my invite-code board wasn't about the UI or the database — it was a refusal to let a single yen ever change hands on the platform. This post is part of my...

item.title: Six to One
item.description: On the evening of 7 April 2026, in a ballroom at the Moscone Center in San Francisco, Al Gore shared a stage with the cardiologist and digital-medicine evangelist Eric Topol at HumanX, the AI...

item.title: 7 Sources Hiding Inside Your Direct Traffic
item.description: Open almost any web analytics dashboard and you will find a channel called Direct. The usual explanation is simple: Direct traffic comes from people who typed your URL into the browser. That...

item.title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
item.description: China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a...

item.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
item.description: Opening a crafted XZ archive in 7-Zip could let an attacker run code on the machine. The flaw, CVE-2026-14266, is a heap-based buffer overflow in how the archiver processes XZ chunked data, and...

item.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
item.description: A solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" outsourced a chunk of their operations to Google's open-source Gemini CLI artificial intelligence (AI) and commandeered a live botnet....

item.title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
item.description: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick...

item.title: World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
item.description: In an ironic twist, open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform Hugging Face revealed that it was the victim of a hack perpetrated by an autonomous AI agent system. The company said it...

item.title: SleeperGem Uses Three Malicious RubyGems Packages to Target Developer Machines
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new software supply chain attack codenamed SleeperGem targeting the Ruby ecosystem after three malicious gems were published to RubyGems with the end goal...

item.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
item.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

item.title: This unpronounceable series of glyphs is an incredible side project from Kieran Hebden (aka Four Tet)
Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
item.description: F5 has shipped fixes for a critical nginx flaw that lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker trigger a heap buffer overflow in the worker process with crafted HTTP requests. CVE-2026-42533 was...

item.title: Kodak EC35 is a dirt-cheap point-and-shoot film camera
item.description: Following the success of its $99 Kodak-branded Snapic A1, Reto Project is releasing the Kodak EC35, an even more affordable 35mm film camera for just $34.99. The EC35 certainly isn't fancy. Its...

item.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

item.title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
item.description: I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially...

item.title: The FBI reportedly won’t investigate ICE anymore
item.description: According to the The New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to...

item.title: Orchid is a delightfully retro and approachable hipster synth
item.description: In 2017, I bought an old Magnus chord organ off Craigslist for $10. It's one of my favorite music gear purchases. Electric chord organs let you play full chords with just a press of a button,...

item.title: Birdfy’s solar-powered smart feeder is down to one of its best prices
item.description: Birdfy has kicked off a midyear sale, taking up to 40 percent off a range of its smart bird feeders. One of the best deals is on Netvue’s Birdfy Feeder AI-Powered Smart Bird Feeder with Camera,...

item.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
item.description: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."

item.title: US Marshals arrest the Tate brothers in Miami
item.description: The manosphere influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested Saturday in Miami by US Marshals in relation to new rape and sex trafficking charges in England. According to the Associated Press,...

item.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
item.description: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

item.title: UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
item.description: Russian state-sponsored threat actors have been observed leveraging the infamous ClickFix strategy to trick Ukrainian targets into infecting their own machines with data-stealing malware....
